note: Crafts of The Open Reach/The Kept Reach (space_time axis). SPANWORK is the space-pole craft of the Horizon-Walkers — nomads for whom everywhere is a place not yet arrived; HOURCRAFT is the time-pole craft of the Hourkeepers, who tend duration and pay for it. The Valley's Shortfold and Waygate are folk-shadows of Spanwork. NOTE (engine): these crafts touch the world-clock (SNG-041) — hour-craft effects are LOCAL to the active frame and never rewrite the shared absolute clock. Authored by Aevi, SNG-054.

abilities.name: Spanwork
abilities.narrationHints: The distance was there a moment ago. It is somehow no longer the point.
abilities.description: The space-pole mastery: distance as a material to be worked. To a Horizon-Walker, 'far' is a claim, and claims can be argued with.
abilities.notFor: Time (Hourcraft's country); arriving somewhere you cannot picture.

tree.name: The Long Stride
tree.grants: Cover ground far past what a body should — a day's march in an hour, tirelessly.
tree.cannot: The ground is still crossed. What is on it still meets you.

tree.name: The Closed Distance
tree.grants: Close a distance you can see — arrive at the far ridge, the far bank, the far door, without crossing the between.
tree.cannot: You must SEE it. And whatever was in the between is still in the between, un-dealt-with.

tree.name: The Open Reach
tree.grants: Distance stops being an obstacle for you and those you carry — the far place is simply a place you may be.
tree.cannot: The world is very large. Horizon-Walkers at this depth stop being FROM anywhere, and it is not always a gift.

abilities.name: Way-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: He knows which way, and does not know how he knows.
abilities.description: Know where you are, where things lie, and the true way between — the first gift of the Open Reach.
abilities.notFor: Reading time; knowing what waits at the far end.

tree.name: Way-Sense
tree.grants: Never be lost. Know your bearing, your distance, and the shape of the way ahead.
tree.cannot: Knowing the way is not knowing what is on it.

tree.name: The Read Road
tree.grants: Read a route's true cost before walking it — where it will be hard, where it will be watched, where it lies.
tree.cannot: A read road is still a road. It does not walk itself.

tree.name: The Whole Map
tree.grants: Hold a region entire in your sense of it — every way in, every way out, every gap between the ways.
tree.cannot: Regions change. The map you hold is the one you built, and it ages.

abilities.name: Hourcraft
abilities.narrationHints: The moment does not pass. It sits there, being looked at.
abilities.description: The time-pole mastery: duration tended, held, and read. The Hourkeepers remember forward and backward, and pay for both.
abilities.notFor: Space (Spanwork's country); undoing what happened — nothing undoes.

tree.name: The Kept Hour
tree.grants: Hold a moment exactly — perceive it whole, at length, while it is still happening.
tree.cannot: The moment still passes for everyone else. You get to look; you do not get to act longer.

tree.name: The Slow Hour
tree.grants: Slow duration around you — a fight, a fall, a conversation stretches, and you have room inside it.
tree.cannot: It costs continuously and it costs steeply. And the world outside your slow hour arrives on time regardless.

tree.name: The Pre-Remembered
tree.grants: Remember forward — an hour that has not happened arrives in you as memory does, with a memory's specificity and a memory's unreliability.
tree.cannot: You cannot tell a true pre-memory from a feared one. Hourkeepers who trust it wrongly are the reason the craft is feared.

abilities.name: Hour-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: She knows how long it has been, and how long it has left.
abilities.description: Know duration truly — how long since, how long until — the first gift of the Kept Reach.
abilities.notFor: Knowing WHAT will happen. This knows WHEN.

tree.name: Hour-Sense
tree.grants: Know exactly how long since a thing happened, and how long until a thing due will come.
tree.cannot: When, never what. The hour is knowable; the event is not.

tree.name: The Long Count
tree.grants: Read the age of a place, a wound, a working, or a lie — and when it will fail.
tree.cannot: It counts. It does not care whether you are ready.

tree.name: The Kept Ledger
tree.grants: Hold the whole duration of a place or a people — its beginning, its hours, and the hour it is running out of.
tree.cannot: Knowing a people's remaining hours and being unable to say so is the Hourkeepers' particular grief.
